attacking_style: Shows capacity for sustained chase efforts and bridging across to lead groups on climbs, but struggles to convert advantages on steep finishing gradients where power-to-weight demands are highest.
team_tactics: Deployed by Movistar as secondary GC option to capitalize on breakaway situations and chase groups, contributing to stage-race depth rather than being primary protected rider.
terrain_preference: Performs best on moderate climbs and in chase scenarios requiring sustained tempo; exposed on steep finishing slopes >10% and technical descents where bike-handling becomes critical.
vulnerability: Cannot maintain threshold power on steepest final 400m finishing ramps; poor descending technique costs positions when chasing (crashed while pursuing on TdF descent).
key_patterns: Fades dramatically in final 400m of steep finishes despite holding advantages earlier (2 observations)
Effective in chase groups and bridging efforts on climbs, positioning for GC gains (2 observations)
Deployed as tactical secondary option rather than protected leader by Movistar (2 observations)
Technical descending weakness: crashes under pressure when chasing (Tour de France 2022 incident)
form_trajectory: neutral: consistent role as chase/bridge specialist across 2022-2023, but recurring inability to finish steep climbs limits breakthrough potential
